section_title: The lender of a thing for use may at any time require its return, if the
content:
159. The lender of a thing for use may at any time require its return, if the
of goods
loan was gratuitous, even though he lent it for a specified time or purpose.

But, if, on the faith of such loan made for a specified time or purpose, the
gratuitously
borrower has acted in such a manner that the return of the thing lent before
the time agreed upon would cause him loss exceeding the benefit actually
derived by him from the loan, the lender must, if he compels the return,
indemnify the borrower for the amount in which the loss so occasioned
exceeds the benefit so derived.
